project_subtitle: Helping you return to missed prayers and fasts with clarity, privacy, and steady progress.
why_body: Awdah was born from a desire to make the journey of making up missed worship feel less overwhelming. We believe in calm, privacy-aware tools that respect the seriousness of your spiritual journey.
who_body: Whether you are returning to practice after a long break or keeping track of a smaller backlog, Awdah gives you structure, visibility, and a gentler way to stay consistent.

features.title: Salah Tracking
features.body: Track missed obligatory prayers with a clear view of what remains and what you've already completed.

features.title: Sawm Tracking
features.body: Log missed Ramadan fasts with dedicated support for qadaa progress and recovery.

features.title: Hijri First
features.body: Built around the Islamic calendar with accurate dual-date support for all entries.

features.title: Bilingual + RTL
features.body: Built for English and Arabic from the start, with full right-to-left support where it belongs.

features.title: Privacy First
features.body: Your data stays yours, with minimal data collection and clear export and deletion controls.

features.title: Learn Hub
features.body: Reference content to explain key rulings, terminology, and product decisions behind qadaa tracking.

team.name: Amgad Mahmoud
team.bio: Cloud Engineer based in Germany with 5 years of experience building serverless software on AWS. Passionate about Clean Architecture, DDD, and building tools that serve the community.
